data.title: Wellgistics Health to acquire Peek Healthcare Technologies
data.description:
• Wellgistics Health (NASDAQ:WGRX) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Peek Healthcare to expand its digital healthcare ecosystem.
  • Upon completion of the transaction, Peek will operate as a wholly-owned subsidiary of Wellgistics Health, maintaining its brand identity while gaining access to an expanded infrastructure, technology resources, and strategic pharmacy relationships.
  • WGRX +5.58% premarket to $4.54.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Arcturus wins FDA fast track status for influenza vaccine
data.description:
• Arcturus Therapeutics (NASDAQ:ARCT) said on Thursday that the U.S. FDA granted fast track designation for its self-amplifying mRNA vaccine candidate, ARCT-2304, against influenza A H5N1 subtype. 
  • Fast-track designation allows a faster timeline for the development and review of drugs targeted at serious conditions with unmet medical needs. It enables developers to communicate frequently with the regulator on plans for clinical studies.
  • Subject to certain conditions, companies with fast-track designations can secure the FDA's accelerated approval and priority review to bring their products to market sooner.
  • The phase 1 clinical study initiated in November 2024, Arcturus said.

data.title: STMicroelectronics unveils measures to tweak footprint, cut costs as pressures rise
data.description:
STMicroelectronics (NYSE:STM) unveiled several measures on Thursday to alter its manufacturing footprint and cut costs as pressures mount over the performance of its Chief Executive Officer, Jean-Marc Chéry.

Shares tumbled 7% in premarket trading.

The French-Italian semiconductor company said it would focus on increasing efficiency, automation, and utilizing artificial intelligence to help strengthen its key technology research and development spending in areas like design, and high-volume assets for advanced manufacturing in Europe.

The company added that its planned investments for fiscal 2025, 2026 and 2027 will focus on advanced manufacturing infrastructure in 300 millimeter silicon, 200 millimeter silicon carbide, and technology R&D.

In addition, STMicro said that it expects approximately 2,800 employees to leave the company on a voluntary basis, on top of normal attrition. It expects to save in the “high” triple-digit million-dollar range, exiting 2027.

“The reshaping of our manufacturing footprint announced today will future-proof our Integrated Device Manufacturer model with strategic assets in Europe and improve our ability to innovate even faster, benefitting all our stakeholders,” Chéry said in a statement. “As we focus on advanced manufacturing infrastructure and mainstream technologies, we will continue to leverage all of our existing sites and bring redefined missions for some of them to support their long-term success.”

The company's activities in Italy and France will “continue to be central to our global operations,” Chéry added.

The moves come as the Italian government withdrew its support for Chéry for “poor performance.”

Tensions between STMicro and the Italian government — which owns a 27.5% stake in the company — spike after it rejected adding Italian government official Marcello Sala to its supervisory board. It was reported last month that Sala was likely to join the supervisory board, whose purpose is to oversee the board of directors and advise it.

Related to that, STMicro's board said on Thursday that it had confidence in Chéry and the rest of the management team. 

STMicro is also jointly owned by the French government, which also owns a 27.5% stake in the European semiconductor company. The analog chip supplier is a joint venture between France and Italy and is the result of a merger of two-state owned companies in 1987.

The company is set to hold its annual shareholder meeting on May 28.

data.title: NUBURU unwinds partnership with HUMBL
data.description:
• NUBURU (BURU) on Thursday announced the unwinding of its previously announced $2M share exchange agreement and partnership with HUMBL (OTCPK:HMBL), effective immediately.
  • NUBURU's management has determined that continuing the partnership no longer aligns with its core business objectives. the company is now refocusing its strategic efforts solely on consolidating its position in the defense and security sectors.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CURRENC regains compliance with Nasdaq
data.description:
• CURRENC (NASDAQ:CURR) said it received written notice from Nasdaq stating that the firm has regained compliance with the minimum market value of publicly held shares requirement.
  • Press Release

data.title: PainReform acquires DeepSolar
data.description:
• PainReform (NASDAQ:PRFX) announced on Thursday the successful acquisition of DeepSolar, a provider of AI-driven software solutions designed to optimize solar energy usage in residential and utility-scale solar power plants.
  • This strategic expansion marks an important milestone in PainReform’s long-term diversification strategy, aimed at tapping into high-growth, tech-enabled markets, said the company.
  • Shares of PRFX jumped +49.45% to $2.72 in premarket trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Conduit Pharmaceuticals announces up to $1M share repurchase program
data.description:
• Conduit Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:CDT) on Thursday announced a share repurchase program of up to $1M of its outstanding common stock.
  • The program has no fixed expiration date and may be adjusted or terminated at any time by the company.
  • CDT +14.51% premarket to $0.71.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stock index futures drops, yields lower as retail inflation comes in cooler
data.description:
Stock index futures fell on Thursday as March CPI came in cooler than expected in March.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -2.3%, and Dow futures (INDU) -1.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 6 basis points to 4.33%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 6 basis points to 3.89%.

The Consumer Price Index came in at -0.1% M/M vs. +0.1% consensus, +2.4% Y/Y vs. +2.6% consensus, and +2.8% prior.

Core CPI came in at +0.1% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior, +2.8% Y/Y vs. +3.0% consensus and +3.1% prior

The European Union paused countermeasures on U.S. tariffs.

It was nothing short of a historic day for markets on Wednesday, as U.S. President Donald Trump's pause on reciprocal tariffs against most countries except China sent Wall Street to its best day since the financial crisis of 2008. The S&P 500 rocketed +9.52%; the Nasdaq Composite exploded +12.16%, and the Dow surged +7.87% on Wednesday. 

"Market turmoil turned into elation yesterday as Trump announced a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for countries other than China, which marked a sizable backing off from last week’s tariff escalation," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.

Trump on Wednesday said he would issue a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for more than 75 countries who had reached out to the U.S. for trade negotiations.

Perhaps most crucially, we are currently still on course for a disorderly economic decoupling between the world’s two largest economies, with no immediate signs of either the U.S. or China backing down, Sidorov said.

Trump also upped the tariff rate against China to 125%. 

Recession risk in the US is lower than yesterday but remains high, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Initial jobless claims edged up to 223K in the past week.

The Fed balance sheet will also land on Thursday.

data.title: Initial jobless claims rise to 223K in past week, meeting consensus
data.description: Initial jobless claims for the week ended April 5 rose by 4K to 223K, matching the consensus and compared with 219K prior (unrevised), according to data released by the U.S. Department of Labor on Thursday.

data.title: Tivic Health Systems regains compliance with Nasdaq minimum bid price listing rule
data.description:
• Tivic Health Systems (NASDAQ:TIVC) on Thursday said it has received confirmation from Nasdaq that the company has regained compliance with the minimum bid requirements.
  • Consequently, Tivic Health’s common stock will continue to be listed and traded on Nasdaq under the symbol “TIVC.”
  • TIVC -3.73% premarket to $4.64.
  • Source: Press Release
